name: Ludger's Roasted Chicken Breast Stuffed with Gorgonzola
recipeIngredient: 4 Chicken breast halves with-skin and bone
5 oz Gorgonzola cheese, mashed, divided into 4 equal parts
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 Green apple, cored, halved, thinly sliced in half moons
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a pocket in each chicken breast. Stuff with the Gorgonzola.
2. Preheat a 12-inch skillet on medium-high heat. Add the olive oil to the pan and place the chicken breasts skin-side down in the pan.
3. Brown the skin and do not turn the chicken. Place in a preheated 350-degree oven for 25 minutes, or until the chicken is done.
4. Remove the chicken from the pan and keep warm. Spoon any melted Gorgonzola left in the pan over the chicken.
5. Pour the fat from the pan and place back on the stove. Add the wine and reduce by a third, about 5-7 minutes.
6. Pour in any juices that have collected around the chicken.
7. When the liquid has thickened, season with salt and pepper and add the grapes and apples. Cook briefly until the apples are just starting to soften.
8. Sprinkle the sauce with parsley and spoon over the chicken. Serve with rice or orzo pasta and steamed vegetables.
4 servings: approximately 25 minutes preparation time
From - "Seattle" - America's Wet Dream
From: Chris Wurgler
